chunk_summary: This section provides low-cost Indian meal templates and audit-ready follow-up goals for addressing chronic undernutrition, focusing on stunting and wasting in children and adolescents through practical dietary interventions and measurable adherence strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand low-cost meal templates for toddlers, school-age children, and adolescents to combat chronic undernutrition.
Learn how to set audit-ready dietary goals for families to improve adherence and track progress.
Recognize the importance of pairing iron-rich foods with vitamin C sources for adolescent girls at risk of anemia.
key_facts: Toddlers (12–23 months): Breakfast with porridge/upma plus oil/ghee and fruit; lunch with khichdi/rice, dal, vegetables, and egg/dairy/soy alternative; snack with milk/curd; dinner with roti/rice, dal, vegetables, and continued breastfeeding.

School-age children: Breakfast with poha/upma and peanuts/milk or roti with egg/curd; after-school snack with roasted chana or peanuts instead of packaged snacks; ensure daily school meal uptake via PM POSHAN scheme.
Adolescents: Pair iron-rich foods with vitamin C sources; prioritize protein-rich breakfasts to improve school function and address anemia risk.
PM POSHAN scheme provides published calorie and protein norms for school meals, which can be used for counseling support.
Follow-up frequency: 2–4 weeks for stunting-only cases initially, then every 6–8 weeks once stable; higher frequency for wasting cases.
reference_quote: PM POSHAN scheme provides published calorie and protein norms for school meals, which can be used for counseling support. (Reference 2)
document_key_topics: Low-cost Indian meal templates for chronic undernutrition
Audit-ready dietary goals for stunting and wasting
PM POSHAN scheme and school meal adherence
Nutritional strategies for anemia prevention in adolescents
